title: Good Things Take Time
lesson: Some good things take time and patience to grow.
prompts: In a sunny backyard garden, warm morning light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left in the mid-ground, holding a small clay pot and a seed packet, facing the camera with a curious smile and the face fully visible. In the right foreground, {GENDER_F_AGE_70_RACE_ASIAN} kneels by a garden bed, gesturing toward the soil. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a sunny backyard garden, warm morning light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image kneels alone pressing a tiny seed into the soil of the clay pot, head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible, with a focused, gentle expression. A small gardening trowel beside them. Eye-level medium shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a sunny backyard garden, warm morning light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image peers closely into the clay pot of bare brown soil with no sprout yet, facing the camera with a disappointed, impatient expression and the face clearly visible. Eye-level medium shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a sunny backyard garden, warm morning light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left in the mid-ground, holding a small watering can, facing the camera with a listening expression and the face fully visible. In the right foreground, {GENDER_F_AGE_70_RACE_ASIAN} smiles warmly and points gently to the clay pot and the sky, as if explaining that plants need time. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a sunny backyard garden, warm morning light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image kneels by the clay pot where a tiny green sprout has just appeared, head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible, eyes wide with delight. A small watering can tips a gentle stream of water. Eye-level medium shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a sunny backyard garden, warm morning light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left holding up the clay pot now with a blooming yellow flower, facing the camera with a proud, joyful smile and the face fully visible. On the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_70_RACE_ASIAN} claps and beams with pride. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
gists: In a sunny backyard garden, the child (far left) holds a small clay pot and a seed packet, curious, while the grandmother at right kneels by a garden bed gesturing toward the soil — time to plant.
In the garden, the child kneels alone pressing a tiny seed into the soil of the clay pot, focused and gentle.
In the garden, the child peers alone into the clay pot of bare soil with no sprout yet, disappointed and impatient.
In the garden, the child (far left) holds a small watering can, listening, while the grandmother at right points to the pot and the sky, explaining plants need time.
In the garden, the child kneels alone by the clay pot where a tiny green sprout has appeared, eyes wide with delight, a watering can tipping a stream of water.
In the garden, the child (far left) holds up the clay pot now with a blooming yellow flower, proud and joyful, while the grandmother at right claps with pride — good things took time.
texts: One sunny morning in the garden, Grandma gave you a little pot and some seeds. "Let's plant!" she said.
You pressed a tiny seed into the soft brown soil, careful and gentle. There — all planted!
You peeked into the pot, but nothing had grown yet. "Where's my flower?" you said, a little sad.
"Plants need time and care," said Grandma, pointing to the sky. So you gave it water and waited.
Then one day — a tiny green sprout! Your eyes went wide. "It's growing!" you cheered.
At last a bright yellow flower bloomed. You held it up high, so proud. Good things take time!
